text: away. Pulses of sharp pain flowed through Lara’s exhausted body, but she ignored them. He was here with her. That was all that mattered . “I need to see her arm,” Nancy said . “No one here means you harm, Lara,” Ronin said, shifting aside to allow Nancy access without breaking contact . Nancy’s cool fingers closed lightly around Lara’s wrist, turning it. She looked over her shoulder, speaking to the others . Lara stared up into Ronin’s eyes. It was only because of his steadiness that she remained still as Nancy softly prodded her hand . “Where are we? Where’s Warlord?” she rasped . “We’re safe. Trust me on that.” He smoothed back her hair. He didn’t look away as Nancy wrapped Lara’s bleeding hand in a bandage . “We’ll be fine,” Nancy said. The other strangers exited through the curtain and pulled it closed . “Look at me,” Ronin said. Lara forced her gaze back up to him. “These people are helping you .” “Where are we, Ronin ?” His fingers trailed along her hairline, down her cheeks, calming her . “Lara,” Nancy said, “I’d like you to drink this. It will help with the pain .” Lara looked at the dark-haired woman and the tiny cup she offered. When she glanced back at Ronin, he nodded. Whoever Nancy was, Ronin trusted her, and that’d have to be enough for now. She took the cup and stared down at the reddish-brown liquid inside. It had a strange odor, similar to the booze at Kitty’s, but something was off about it. Her instincts said don’t drink it . “Best to swallow it quickly,” Nancy said, offering a reassuring smile . Raising the cup to her lips, Lara hesitated for a second before pouring it into her mouth and swallowing quickly . It burned like fire going down her throat. The flavor of the alcohol fled quickly, overpowered by a bitter aftertaste worse than anything she’d ever had. She coughed, wincing, and pain exploded through her chest . “What the hell was that?” she demanded through clenched teeth, taking shallow breaths. Anything deeper produced a sharp pain in her side . “Laudanum, or as close to it as we can manage. Our options for pain management are limited, but most people say the relief is worth the taste, once it kicks in.” Nancy settled a soft hand on Lara’s shoulder. “Get some rest. You’ve been through a lot, but the worst is over. All you have to do now is heal. I’ll check on you a little later .” Nancy walked away, slipping through the curtain . “It hurts so much,” she whispered, touching her side . The bed creaked as Ronin sat on the edge, placing his hand over Lara’s. With his other hand, he delicately ran his fingertips over her cheek, brushing strands of hair behind her ear. “I thought I was going to lose you, Lara Brooks .” Lara met his gaze. She’d never seen such agony in his eyes. She turned her hand over and laced her fingers with his. Her memories of what had happened were unclear, but she’d heard the gunshots, had watched the light fade from his gaze . “I did lose you,” she said, voice hoarse as her throat tightened and tears welled in her eyes . His smile was sorrow-tinged, but genuine. “Now I understand helplessness and fear .” The tears rolled down her cheeks. “I love you .” “Love you, too.” He wiped away the moisture with his thumbs. “Rest. I’ll be here when you wake again, and I’ll tell you everything .” Though she longed to stay awake, to stare at him and touch him and hear his voice, the laudanum was taking effect. The aches that wracked her body dulled, and the edges of her consciousness grew fuzzy. The bedding dipped, and Ronin’s warmth enveloped her as he laid down beside her . Sleep claimed Lara . * * * “W hat’s wrong?” Ronin asked . Lara looked up from the bowl in his hand, dropping the spoon into it. She’d spent two days in constant discomfort and pain. The drowsiness and disorientation caused by the laudanum had lingered a long while after she woke, and she refused more when Nancy brought it. She wanted to remain awake and alert. Wanted the reminder that she was alive and healing . “Why didn’t Warlord kill me ?” The skin between Ronin’s brows creased. “As far as he was concerned, he did .” “He hunted us down like animals. Why not finish it? He killed Tabitha.” Her throat constricted. “Why not me , too ?” “What he did to Tabitha was meant as an example to everyone in Cheyenne. She…suffered, but he ended it. With us, he was only concerned about the suffering. There was no one around to witness it, and no one from Cheyenne would ever find us there. He wanted us broken, wanted you to die slowly and in pain, all alone .” The four, quick gunshots rang out in her memory. If it hadn’t been for Newton and the spare power supplies in the shelter, she’d be dead in the Dust. The twenty feet between her and Ronin’s deactivated casing might as well have been a thousand miles . “What did he do to her?” Lara asked quietly. “What example did he make ?” He was quiet, eyes dropping . “Ronin. I deserve to know .” For a few more seconds, she didn’t think he’d reply. “They beat her to death. Her body was covered in bruises, her facial bones shattered, blood matting her hair. After they dismantled the synth, they laid his face over her groin and put his penis in her mouth .” She grabbed his wrist, and he lifted his gaze to meet hers. The sudden action sent stabbing pain through her side. Despite the bindings around her torso, it still hurt like a son of a bitch. “Why didn’t you tell me,
